news.news: Cartherics and Catalent expanded their commercial license agreement to utilize a Catalent cGMP-compliant induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC) line for manufacturing CAR-NK cell therapies. The partnership leverages Catalent's validated workflows for gene editing to support the development of Cartherics' lead product, CTH-401, for ovarian cancer and endometriosis.

Généthon scientists published a study in Molecular Therapy detailing a CRISPR/Cas9 strategy to increase utrophin expression in Duchenne muscular dystrophy by disabling a microRNA binding site. The approach demonstrated improved muscle function and reduced fibrosis in preclinical models, offering a potential universal therapy independent of specific DMD mutations.
